ops.text:
PRE-REGISTERED PROTOCOL (opening entry; Wave-2 arm E29).

GOAL: SFT a single-letter pick-predictor on E24's AskUserQuestion decisions and test whether it clears E24's difficulty floor on the identical session-held-out eval.

DATA: /Users/yasyf/.cc-steer/experiments/E24/out/decisions.jsonl (2018 clean mirror decisions; verified). E29 excludes the 120 multi-select rows (load_decisions filters multi_select) -> 1898 single-select decisions. Each single-select row is a K+1-way problem over {option_0..option_{K-1}, custom/Other}; Other is E24's ~24% deferral class.

SPLIT (E24-parity, apples-to-apples): group-by-session, test iff int(sha256(session_id),16) % 10 == 0 (e29lib.session_split == e24_common.split_of). Confirmed on real data: train=1696, test=202 single-select (== E24's exact 202 single-select eval rows), 460 sessions, zero session straddle. val carved from train by hashing session_id+"#val" (val_frac 0.15). from_record maps E24 field names (picks[0].{is_custom,option_index}, options[list[str]], header, question, context_digest, session_id, tool_use_id, question_index) -> Decision; verified against a real record.

MODEL: qwen3-8b base, SFT LoRA (incumbent WatcherRecipe.default()), single-letter classifier (system+question+options -> chosen letter). Checkpoint selected by val single-select accuracy over option-letter first-token logprobs (CheckpointPolicy at=0.5 + final). One paid launch does fit+select+score under a single $9 SpendGuard envelope (athome.train.observe).

METRICS (E24-exact, via e24_common.single_metrics): accuracy, bootstrap 95% CI (1000 resamples, seed 24), 15-bin confidence-ECE (cc_steer.retrain.lexical.ece), Brier (multiclass + top-label). Floor = always-predict-option-0 = 0.5743 (116/202). E24 best baseline: acc 0.579, always-opt-0 prior 0.574, best ECE 0.026.

PRE-REGISTERED GATE (E29): the FT model is promising ONLY IF single-select accuracy 95% CI lower bound > 0.574 (CI excludes the floor) AND ECE <= ~0.10. Reported as gate_met bool. This gate stands on human ground-truth picks, so the sentinel-AUC instrument card (MDE/DeLong) does NOT gate it; the relevant test is the paired-bootstrap accuracy CI vs the trivial floor + the ECE bound.

STEP 2 (SUPPLEMENTAL cross-corpus transfer): all 18 /Users/yasyf/.cc-pool/accounts/acct-*/projects symlink to the single local /Users/yasyf/.claude/projects (35 project dirs) -- RECONFIRMS E24 CARD.md caveat that pool accounts are not distinct claude-homes. So the "cross-corpus" set = local-home AskUserQuestion decisions (extracted once via E24's extract_decisions.py over /Users/yasyf/.claude/projects), deduped against the 2018 mirror set by (session_id, tool_use_id), held out ENTIRELY (never trained), scored under the SAME FT checkpoint. Report: size, FT accuracy, always-opt-0 floor accuracy. Flagged as a shape note in the return: the local corpus overlaps/updates the mirror rather than being a foreign corpus.

ARTIFACTS to persist (out/ under E29): prep_report.json, pool_raw.jsonl + pool_eval.jsonl (deduped), preds_test.jsonl (E24-test per-row probs), preds_pool.jsonl (pool per-row probs), best checkpoint tinker:// path, e29_report.json, e29_score gate JSON. Raw per-row probs kept for a verify lane to re-derive.
